title: Ratio of same-day trips to tourist trips
subtitle: Same-day trips are those by visitors who arrive and depart within the same day. Tourist trips are those by people who stay overnight. A country with a ratio greater than one has more trips by visitors who leave the same day than those that stay overnight.
caption:
Ratio of same-day trips to tourist trips

Same-day trips are those by visitors who arrive and depart within the same day. Tourist trips are those by people who stay overnight. A country with a ratio greater than one has more trips by visitors who leave the same day than those that stay overnight.

This choropleth world map displays the ratio of same-day trips to tourist trips for the year 2024. The legend ranges from 0 to 4, with color bands shifting from light green for lower ratios to dark purple for higher ratios, alongside a hatched pattern indicating no data. Countries with ratios greater than one, meaning same-day visits exceed overnight stays, appear in shades of purple and pink. The United States, Canada, and Saudi Arabia sit in the light purple band, corresponding to a ratio between 1 and 2. In Europe, France and the United Kingdom also fall within this 1 to 2 range, while Germany and parts of Eastern Europe appear in darker purple shades, indicating ratios between 2 and 3 or higher. Conversely, many nations in South America, Africa, and Asia display light green shades, signifying ratios between 0 and 0.5, where overnight tourist trips significantly outnumber same-day visits. India, China, and Australia are covered in diagonal hatching, denoting that data is not available for these regions. Similarly, large portions of Africa and Central Asia show this hatched pattern. The visual distribution highlights a contrast between North American and certain European nations, which show higher proportions of same-day travel, and many developing regions where overnight tourism is more prevalent or data is missing.
